Benchmark Results

Source: vignettes/benchmark-results.Rmd
benchmark-results.Rmd

This vignette compares eight context-retrieval strategies implemented in rrlmgraph-bench on a suite of realistic R-project tasks. Results were pre-computed by the run-benchmark GitHub Actions workflow in rrlmgraph-bench and summarised here — no benchmark re-run is needed to render this page.

Strategies

Strategy Description
full_files Entire source files (baseline)
bm25 BM25 keyword retrieval
tfidf TF-IDF cosine similarity
graph_bfs Unweighted BFS from top-scoring nodes
graph_pagerank PageRank-weighted BFS
graph_combined PageRank + TF-IDF hybrid
rrlmgraph Full rrlmgraph relevance pipeline
rrlmgraph_mcp rrlmgraph via MCP server subprocess

Results 

# When a vignette is built, cwd = vignettes/ so this relative path works
# both during R CMD check and pkgdown site builds.
precomputed_path <- file.path("precomputed", "benchmark_summary.rds")
if (file.exists(precomputed_path)) {
    bench <- readRDS(precomputed_path)
} else {
    bench <- NULL
}
if (is.null(bench)) {
    cat(
        "> **Note:** Benchmark results not yet available.\n",
        "> Trigger the `run-benchmark` workflow in",
        "[rrlmgraph-bench](https://github.com/davidrsch/rrlmgraph-bench/actions)",
        "to generate data, then run the `precompute-vignettes` workflow here.\n"
    )
}

Note: Benchmark results not yet available. Trigger the run-benchmark workflow in rrlmgraph-bench to generate data, then run the precompute-vignettes workflow here.

stats <- bench$stats
knitr::kable(
    stats$summary[, c(
        "strategy", "mean_score", "sd_score", "ci_lower", "ci_upper",
        "mean_tokens", "hallucination_rate", "n_obs"
    )],
    digits = 3,
    caption = paste0(
        "Mean score ± 95 % CI per strategy.  ",
        "Captured: ", bench$meta$generated_at, ".  ",
        "rrlmgraph v", bench$meta$rrlmgraph_version, " / ",
        "rrlmgraphbench v", bench$meta$rrlmgraphbench_version, "."
    ),
    col.names = c(
        "Strategy", "Mean score", "SD", "CI lower", "CI upper",
        "Mean tokens", "Hallucination rate", "N"
    )
)
stats <- bench$stats
df <- stats$summary
df$strategy <- factor(df$strategy, levels = df$strategy[order(df$mean_score)])
baseline <- df$mean_score[df$strategy == "full_files"]

op <- par(mar = c(5, 10, 3, 2))
plot(
    df$mean_score,
    seq_len(nrow(df)),
    xlim = c(0, 1),
    xlab = "Mean score",
    ylab = "",
    yaxt = "n",
    pch = 16,
    col = ifelse(df$strategy == "rrlmgraph", "steelblue",
        ifelse(df$strategy == "rrlmgraph_mcp", "seagreen", "grey40")
    ),
    main = "Strategy comparison"
)
axis(2, at = seq_len(nrow(df)), labels = df$strategy, las = 1, cex.axis = 0.85)
abline(v = baseline, lty = 2, col = "grey60")
arrows(
    df$ci_lower, seq_len(nrow(df)),
    df$ci_upper, seq_len(nrow(df)),
    length = 0.05, angle = 90, code = 3,
    col = ifelse(df$strategy == "rrlmgraph", "steelblue",
        ifelse(df$strategy == "rrlmgraph_mcp", "seagreen", "grey40")
    )
)
par(op)

Token efficiency

The Token Efficiency Ratio (TER) measures score gain per token relative to the full_files baseline. Values > 1 indicate better score per token used.

ter_df <- data.frame(
    strategy = names(bench$stats$ter),
    TER = round(bench$stats$ter, 3),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
knitr::kable(ter_df, row.names = FALSE, caption = "Token Efficiency Ratio (TER)")

Pairwise tests 

pw <- bench$stats$pairwise
knitr::kable(
    pw[, c("strategy_a", "strategy_b", "p_bonferroni", "cohens_d", "significant")],
    digits    = 4,
    col.names = c("Strategy A", "Strategy B", "p (Bonferroni)", "Cohen's d", "Sig."),
    caption   = "Pairwise Welch t-tests (Bonferroni-corrected)."
)

Methodology

Full task definitions and scoring rubrics are in the rrlmgraph-bench repository. Each task is scored 0–1 using compute_benchmark_statistics() from the rrlmgraphbench package. Hallucinations are detected by detect_hallucination().

On this page

Developed by David Díaz.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
